h_mech_factorial.conclusion: Encoder-driven absorption CONFIRMED (encoder effect 80x larger than decoder)

multiseed_validation.conclusion: Trained SAEs show significantly higher absorption (p=3.85e-10)

h3_steering.conclusion: No significant difference in steering sensitivity (negative result)

h_safe_gemma.conclusion: No difference between safety and non-safety features (negative result)

ablation_l0_sparsity.conclusion: Lower sparsity -> higher absorption (opposite of hypothesis)

confirmed_findings: Trained SAEs show higher absorption than random (H1)
negative_findings: Steering does not differentially affect absorbed features (H3)
Safety features are not disproportionately absorbed (H_Safe)
Lower sparsity leads to higher absorption (opposite of hypothesis)
overall_assessment.interpretation: Absorption is a fundamental property of encoder training in SAEs, driven by encoder alignment with hierarchical structure. The effect is robust across seeds and generalizes to new data. However, absorbed features are not functionally different from non-absorbed in terms of steering sensitivity, and safety features are not special.
